data.title: As a DSO agent running design review, I can query 48 government UI/UX anti-patterns to identify potential design problems in the work being reviewed
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Populates plugins/dso/data/ui-reference/anti-patterns/ with all 48 government UI/UX anti-patterns from epic 17de-a47a, formatted as Markdown files with YAML frontmatter and three resolution tiers. Manually updates _index.yaml with all new entries.

**Why**: Government-specific anti-patterns are the highest-value corpus content for design review agents — they directly address patterns seen in federal digital services. This story absorbs epic 17de-a47a's content into the corpus, making it retrievable via ref-query.sh.

**Scope**:
- IN: All 48 anti-pattern entries from epic 17de-a47a; anti-patterns/ domain directory files (≤500 lines each); _index.yaml manually updated with all 48 new entries; domain tag values anchored to _schema.yaml vocabulary (auth/forms/navigation/visual per content type)
- OUT: Anti-patterns from WCAG, USWDS, or Brignull taxonomy (belong in Stories 3-4 and 5b); ref-query.sh changes; _schema.yaml changes

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, anti-patterns/ domain directory contains all 48 entries from epic 17de-a47a; each entry has valid YAML frontmatter conforming to _schema.yaml vocabulary and Summary + Detail tiers; no entry file exceeds 500 lines
  ← Satisfies: "Initial corpus covers the full set identified during research: government anti-patterns (all 48 from 17de-a47a at Detail tier...)" (SC4)

- When this story is complete, anti-pattern domain tag values are anchored: session/authentication patterns use domain: [auth], form-related patterns use domain: [forms], navigation patterns use domain: [navigation], visual/display patterns use domain: [visual]; these canonical tag choices are documented in a comment at the top of each anti-patterns/ file
  ← Satisfies: SC4 + Story-6 precision query contract (government authentication anti-pattern query expects domain: [auth])

- When this story is complete, _index.yaml is manually updated with all 48 new entries and committed; check-corpus-schema.sh --validate-index confirms all new entries resolve to valid files
  ← Satisfies: SC2 (_index.yaml reflects all committed entries)

- When this story is complete, check-corpus-schema.sh passes on all anti-patterns/ files with no tag vocabulary violations
  ← Satisfies: SC1

- When this story is complete, dso ref-query "session timeout" returns at least one anti-patterns entry with domain: [auth] in its frontmatter
  ← Satisfies: SC4 + SC3 (retrieval works for anti-patterns content)

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Anti-pattern entry IDs must be unique across the full corpus — use prefix ap- (e.g., id: ap-session-timeout-missing)
- [Reliability] After this story completes, add a comment to epic 17de-a47a ticket noting its content has been absorbed; 17de-a47a can be closed as superseded when this epic completes

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
